assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: Task 1 (trustify-backend), Task 2 (trustify-backend), Task 3 (trustify-backend), Task 4 (trustify-backend), Task 5 (trustify-backend), Task 10 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: Task 6 (trustify-ui), Task 7 (trustify-ui), Task 8 (trustify-ui), Task 9 (trustify-ui). Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are numbered 1-5 and 10. Frontend tasks are numbered 6-9. All backend implementation tasks (2-5) have lower numbers than frontend tasks (6-9). Task 10 is a merge-branch bookend that depends on all others, which is valid.

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 7 (SbomComparePage, UI-facing) references Figma design ('per the Figma design' in description) and lists specific PatternFly components: Select, ExpandableSection, Badge, Table, EmptyState, CodeBranchIcon, Skeleton, Dropdown. Task 9 (Compare selected action on SbomListPage, UI-facing) references PatternFly's composable Table row selection feature. Task 6 (API types/client/hook) and Task 8 (MSW mocks) are API-layer tasks and are exempt.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: All tasks use feature-branch workflow with Target Branch TC-9003 (intermediate tasks) or main (bookend tasks). Backend implementation tasks are numbered 2-5 and frontend tasks 6-9, establishing implicit ordering. Additionally, the impact map states: 'The frontend comparison page requires the new backend GET /api/v2/sbom/compare endpoint' confirming the cross-repo dependency. While no frontend task explicitly lists a backend task in its Dependencies section, the feature-branch workflow with lower backend task numbers satisfies the alternative criterion.

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md: Task 2 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs, details.rs, mod.rs; Task 3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/error.rs, entity/src/sbom_package.rs; Task 4 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs, list.rs, mod.rs; Task 5 references tests/api/sbom.rs. Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md: Task 6 references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/api/client.ts, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/hooks/useSbomById.ts; Task 7 references src/routes.tsx, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx; Task 8 references tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/mocks/fixtures/sboms.json; Task 9 references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, src/components/FilterToolbar.tsx. All paths match the respective repo structure manifests.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 tasks have a '## Repository' section with a single repo name: Tasks 1-5 and 10 have 'trustify-backend', Tasks 6-9 have 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 10 tasks contain: ## Target Branch (task 1: main, tasks 2-9: TC-9003, task 10: main), ## Description (present in all), ## Acceptance Criteria (present in all with checkbox items), ## Test Requirements (present in all with checkbox items).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Backend task file references: Task 2 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs (new file under existing model/ dir from repo-backend.md), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (exists in repo-backend.md). Task 3 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/comparison.rs (new file under existing service/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs (exists). Task 4 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/compare.rs (new under existing endpoints/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs and server/src/main.rs (both exist). Task 5 creates tests/api/sbom_compare.rs (new under existing tests/api/ dir). Frontend tasks: Task 6 modifies src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts (both exist in repo-frontend.md), creates src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts (new under existing hooks/ dir). Task 7 creates files under src/pages/SbomComparePage/ (new page dir following existing pattern), modifies src/routes.tsx and src/App.tsx (both exist). Task 8 modifies tests/mocks/handlers.ts (exists), creates tests/mocks/fixtures/sbom-comparison.json (new fixture in existing dir). Task 9 modifies src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (exists). All paths are consistent with the repo structures.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Tasks 1 (create-branch) and 10 (merge-branch) are bookend tasks and omit Implementation Notes, which is correct. All non-bookend tasks (2-9) have Implementation Notes with specific file paths: Task 2 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs and details.rs; Task 3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, PackageService, AdvisoryService with paths, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/error.rs; Task 4 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs, list.rs, mod.rs; Task 5 references tests/api/sbom.rs; Task 6 references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/api/client.ts, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/hooks/useSbomById.ts; Task 7 references src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, src/pages/SbomDetailPage/SbomDetailPage.tsx, src/utils/severityUtils.ts; Task 8 references tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/mocks/fixtures/sboms.json; Task 9 references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, src/components/FilterToolbar.tsx.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks (Task 1 create-branch and Task 10 merge-branch) have Target Branch: main. All intermediate tasks (2-9) have Target Branch: TC-9003 (the feature issue ID). This matches the feature-branch workflow pattern exactly.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: No sha256 digest comments were found in any of the output files. Searched all 11 files for 'sha256', 'digest', and '[sdlc-workflow]' — no matches. The outputs contain only task description markdown files and an impact map, with no evidence of digest comments being posted.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention applicability rationales were found in any task's Implementation Notes. Searched all output files for 'Applies:', 'CONVENTIONS.md', and 'convention' — no matches in the prescribed format. The word 'convention' appears only in generic context (e.g., 'TypeScript convention in this codebase', 'struct definition conventions', 'naming conventions') but never in the 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §...' format with an 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' rationale. No evidence of convention-aware enrichment being performed.
